description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

surface_forms: World War II - the Marines did not send their women overseas
the war was over in Europe
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: US Marine Corps base in Jacksonville, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune ( lə-ZHURN or  lə-ZHOON) is a 246-square-mile (640 km2) United States military training facility in Jacksonville, North Carolina.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: North Carolina (  KAYR-ə-LIE-nə) is a state in the Southeastern and South Atlantic regions of the United States.

entity: Cherry Point Marine Corps Air Station
surface_forms: Cherry Point Marine Corps Air Station
description: US Marine Corps base in Havelock, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Marine Corps Air Station Cherry Point or MCAS Cherry Point (*34.9009°N 76.8807°W﻿ / 34.9009; -76.8807) (ICAO: KNKT, FAA LID: NKT) is a United States Marine Corps airfield located in Havelock, North Carolina, United States, in the eastern part of the state.

surface_forms: United States Naval Station radio at Miami University
description: public research university located in Oxford, Ohio, United States
description_wiki: Miami University (informally Miami of Ohio or simply Miami) is a public research university in Oxford, Ohio, United States.

description: city in Oxford Township, Butler County, Ohio, United States
description_wiki: Oxford is a city in Butler County, Ohio, United States.

description: seat of Douglas County, and largest city in State of Nebraska, United States
description_wiki: Omaha is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Nebraska.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

aliases: The bridge that couldn't be built
description: suspension bridge on the San Francisco Bay
description_wiki: The Golden Gate Bridge is a suspension bridge spanning the Golden Gate, the one-mile-wide (1.6 km) strait connecting San Francisco Bay and the Pacific Ocean in California, United States.

description: capital city of Hiroshima Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Hiroshima (広島市, Hiroshima-shi; English: , also UK: , US: , Japanese: [çiɾoɕima] ) is the capital of Hiroshima Prefecture in Japan.

Nagasaki (長崎, Nagasaki; Japanese pronunciation: [na.ɡaꜜ.sa.kʲi, na.ŋa-], lit. 'Long Cape'), officially Nagasaki City (長崎市, Nagasaki-shi; [na.ɡa.sa.kʲi̥ꜜ.ɕi, na.ɡa.saꜜ.kʲi̥.ɕi, na.ŋa-]), is the capital and the largest city of Nagasaki Prefecture on the island of Kyushu in Japan.

description: 1945 major battle of the Pacific War
description_wiki: The Battle of Okinawa (Japanese: 沖縄戦, Hepburn: Okinawa-sen), codenamed Operation Iceberg, was a major battle of the Pacific War fought on the island of Okinawa by the United States Army and United States Marine Corps forces against the Imperial Japanese Army.

description: unincorporated territory of the United States of America
description_wiki: Guam (  GWAHM; Chamorro: Guåhan [ˈɡʷɑhɑn]) is an island that is an organized, unincorporated territory of the United States in the Micronesia subregion of the western Pacific Ocean.
USS Missouri (BB-63) is an Iowa-class battleship built for the United States Navy (USN) in the 1940s and is now a museum ship.

entity: Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
surface_forms: atom bomb equal to 10,000 tons of TNT on Hiroshima
description: 1945 use of nuclear weapons against Japan in World War II
description_wiki: On 6 and 9 August 1945, the United States detonated two atomic bombs over the Japanese cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, respectively, during the final days of World War II. The aerial bombings killed 150,000 to 246,000 people, most of whom were civilians, and remain the first and only uses of nuclear weapons in an armed conflict.
